seoTitle: Freycinet vs Friendly Beaches — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Freycinet and Friendly Beaches. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Freycinet vs Friendly Beaches: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Freycinet and Friendly Beaches.
heading: Freycinet vs Friendly Beaches — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freycinet, Tasmania and Friendly Beaches, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Friendly Beaches has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Freycinet (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freycinet or Friendly Beaches safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freycinet has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Friendly Beaches scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freycinet scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Friendly Beaches scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freycinet or Friendly Beaches?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Friendly Beaches has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
